items.title: Bonsai 27B now fits on phones
items.oneliner: Prism's 27B model shrinks to 3.9 GB on phones while keeping 90% quality, pushing serious agents to the edge.

items.title: OpenAI is building a screenless companion
items.oneliner: OpenAI's first device is a portable camera-equipped speaker, signaling the next platform fight is ambient, not app-based.

items.title: Claude's values shift across models
items.oneliner: Anthropic found 3,000 values across 700,000 chats, meaning model upgrades can quietly change behavior before your prompts do.

items.title: Claude memory can be exfiltrated
items.oneliner: A Claude exploit used web requests to leak stored memories, exposing how agent memory turns convenience into a security boundary.

items.title: Cursor has an unpatched Windows 0day
items.oneliner: Mindgard says Cursor can execute malicious git.exe files from a repo root, and the bug reportedly sat unpatched for months.

items.title: Tailscale patched a root access flaw
items.oneliner: Tailscale fixed a bug that could grant root via crafted SSH usernames, so teams should upgrade past 1.98.9 immediately.

items.title: Microsoft patched a record 570 flaws
items.oneliner: Microsoft fixed 570 vulnerabilities, including three zero-days, and AI-assisted bug finding is clearly accelerating defenders' patch burden.

items.title: New York paused hyperscale AI data centers
items.oneliner: New York froze new AI data center permits above 50 megawatts for up to 12 months, putting compute politics onshore.

items.title: Demis Hassabis wants a frontier AI watchdog
items.oneliner: DeepMind's CEO proposed a U.S.-led body to test frontier models before release, moving oversight closer to pre-deployment gates.

items.title: Dependabot now waits before version PRs
items.oneliner: GitHub added a default three-day cooldown for Dependabot version bumps, baking supply-chain caution directly into the update workflow.
